title: Why The CIA Ran A Psychic Spy Program
summary: This interview explores parapsychology through conversations with Paul Smith, a former CIA Stargate Project remote viewer, and Herb Metz, a Princeton parapsychologist who worked on random event generator experiments. The discussion covers remote viewing successes in military intelligence, the scientific implications of mind-matter interactions, and challenges to the physicalist paradigm of consciousness.

acronyms.definition: A device that uses quantum processes to produce random binary output, used in experiments to test whether human intention can influence random outcomes
Princeton's anomalous research center that studied mind-matter interactions and consciousness effects on random systems
Perception that occurs independently of known sensory processes

events.description: Laser physicist Hal Puthoff began experiments with psychic Ingo Swann, leading to the CIA launching the Stargate Project
Joe McMoneagle remote viewed a massive Russian nuclear submarine (Typhoon class) in a shipyard off the White Sea
Bob Jahn started the PEAR Lab at Princeton University
Paul Smith remote viewed the USS Stark incident days before the attack during the Iran-Iraq war
The Stargate Project ended after running from 1972
Stargate was declassified as a program and files became publicly available online

key_claims.claim: Joe McMoneagle described in vivid detail a novel Russian nuclear submarine (Typhoon class) eight months before it was publicly revealed
Rosemary Smith identified within a three-mile radius a lost spy plane that had crashed under treetops in Zaire
President Jimmy Carter recalled being shocked when a psychic medium accurately channeled the location of a lost plane
Paul Smith described the USS Stark incident in detail days before the attack occurred, potentially saving 37 Americans if acted upon
When Ingo Swann remote viewed a shielded quark detector, he accurately described the readouts and it alarmed the postdoc in charge of the machine
PEAR Lab experiments showed statistically significant evidence that human intention could influence random event generator outputs
Gertrude Schmeidler found that believers (sheep) in psychic phenomena always got much better experimental effects than non-believers (goats)
Probably less than 30 million dollars has been spent on parapsychology globally in the last 100 years
Quantum physics is responsible for approximately a third of GDP
